authoritative_sources.title: National Incident Management System: Command and Coordination
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Fire Administration and Federal Emergency Management Agency
authoritative_sources.source_class: Statutes, agency manuals, or public-program guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FEMA and USFA publish official NIMS command and coordination guidance. It is authoritative for public-sector multiagency coordination concepts, common terminology, multijurisdictional efforts, policy groups, information sharing, and coordination structures.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Interagency intake, coordination structure selection, policy-owner identification, and receiving-owner readiness
used_for: boundary between coordination support and senior policy decision making

authoritative_sources.title: Federal Acquisition Regulation Subpart 17.5, Interagency Acquisitions
authoritative_sources.source_class: Records, procurement, or public-service operating rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The FAR is the primary federal acquisition regulation, and Subpart 17.5 directly governs interagency acquisitions, requesting and servicing agency roles, determinations, Economy Act orders, and acquisition approval boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Interagency procurement or service-order prerequisite validation and owner routing
used_for: refusal to obligate funds or approve acquisition strategy

authoritative_sources.title: 31 U.S.C. 1535, Agency Agreements
authoritative_sources.publisher: Office of the Law Revision Counsel, U.S. House of Representatives
authoritative_sources.source_class: Statutes, agency manuals, or public-program guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The Economy Act is statutory authority for covered federal agency agreements. It is directly relevant where interagency coordination touches orders, supplies, services, authority to obtain work from another agency, and payment prerequisites.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: IAA or service-order authority check before handoff into procurement or fulfillment

authoritative_sources.title: G-Invoicing: Frequently Asked Questions - About Moving to G-Invoicing
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of the Treasury, Bureau of the Fiscal Service
authoritative_sources.source_class: Records, procurement, or public-service operating rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The Bureau of the Fiscal Service operates G-Invoicing and publishes official guidance for federal intragovernmental buy/sell transaction data exchange, GT&Cs, orders, performance, settlement, official accounting-source boundaries, and audit trail behavior.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: GT&C, order, performance, trading-partner communication, and audit-trail handling for IAA-adjacent workflows
used_for: audit trail and version comparison expectations

authoritative_sources.title: 44 U.S.C. 3101, Records management by agency heads; general duties
authoritative_sources.publisher: Office of the Law Revision Counsel, U.S. House of Representatives
authoritative_sources.source_class: Records, procurement, or public-service operating rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This statute establishes duties to make and preserve records that document agency organization, functions, policies, decisions, procedures, and essential transactions. It directly constrains official record expectations for interagency handoffs.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Official record, correspondence, decision, and transaction documentation validation
used_for: auditability of public decisions and essential transactions

authoritative_sources.title: 36 CFR Part 1222, Creation and Maintenance of Federal Records
authoritative_sources.publisher: Electronic Code of Federal Regulations and National Archives and Records Administration
authoritative_sources.source_class: Records, procurement, or public-service operating rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NARA's records regulation is authoritative for federal record creation, maintenance, documentation, electronic records, and recordkeeping controls that determine whether a handoff packet is auditable and complete.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Record creation, maintenance, documentation completeness, and system-of-record validation

authoritative_sources.title: 5 U.S.C. 552, Public information; agency rules, opinions, orders, records, and proceedings
authoritative_sources.publisher: Office of the Law Revision Counsel, U.S. House of Representatives
authoritative_sources.source_class: Records, procurement, or public-service operating rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FOIA is the federal public-records transparency baseline. It is authoritative for public inspection, records request procedures, consultation with interested agencies, timing, appeals, and disclosure-sensitive handoffs.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Public-records transparency, correspondence, consultation, and disclosure-risk routing
used_for: boundary against disclosure or redaction decisions

authoritative_sources.title: 5 U.S.C. 552a, Records maintained on individuals
authoritative_sources.publisher: Office of the Law Revision Counsel, U.S. House of Representatives
authoritative_sources.source_class: Records, procurement, or public-service operating rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The Privacy Act is authoritative for federal systems of records about individuals, disclosure accounting, amendment requests, routine-use boundaries, and interagency sharing of privacy-sensitive records.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Privacy-sensitive referral packet validation and disclosure-accounting checks

authoritative_sources.title: 5 U.S.C. 554, Adjudications
authoritative_sources.publisher: Office of the Law Revision Counsel, U.S. House of Representatives
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The APA adjudication provision is authoritative for formal federal administrative adjudication procedures and is directly relevant when an interagency handoff would affect rights, benefits, licensing, adjudication, or official determinations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Rights-sensitive exception handling and adjudication-boundary escalation

authoritative_sources.title: Standards for Internal Control in the Federal Government
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GAO's Green Book is the federal internal-control standard. It is authoritative for documentation, information quality, control activities, monitoring, and accountability expectations in auditable government workflows.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Dependency control, auditability, blocker escalation, and closure evidence review

workflow_stages.name: Dependency intake and authority framing
workflow_stages.description: Confirm requester, program, jurisdiction, statute or policy basis, current owner, receiving agency, requested outcome, service population, deadline, MOU or IAA reference, and whether the lane owns coordination rather than the underlying public decision.
source_classes: Statutes, agency manuals, or public-program guidance
Records, procurement, or public-service operating rules

workflow_stages.name: Source record and prerequisite validation
workflow_stages.description: Validate case file, referral packet, memorandum, correspondence history, notices, approval tracker, public-records and privacy caveats, procurement or funding artifacts, official record location, system audit log, and receiving-owner readiness before handoff.
source_classes: Records, procurement, or public-service operating rules

workflow_stages.name: Cross-agency routing and evidence handoff
workflow_stages.description: Prepare a handoff summary, referral packet index, owner map, missing-prerequisite list, routing note, and transfer confirmation using documented handoff rules, agency agreements, program manuals, and system-of-record references.
source_classes: Statutes, agency manuals, or public-program guidance

workflow_stages.name: Blocker escalation and conflict handling
workflow_stages.description: Escalate when records are incomplete, official systems conflict, agency ownership is ambiguous, authorities or policies conflict, privacy or disclosure basis is unclear, procurement or IAA prerequisites are missing, or the request crosses into adjudication, enforcement, legal, fiscal, or procurement authority.
source_classes: Statutes, agency manuals, or public-program guidance
Records, procurement, or public-service operating rules

workflow_stages.name: Closure confirmation and audit preservation
workflow_stages.description: Confirm that the receiving owner accepted the handoff, required records landed in the correct systems, unresolved blockers are logged, stale ownership is escalated, and closure language does not overstate decision authority.
source_classes: Records, procurement, or public-service operating rules

artifacts: interagency request, referral form, memorandum, MOU, interagency agreement, GT&C, order, program manual excerpt, and statutory or policy authority reference
case file, eligibility record, notices, appeal or due-process deadlines, service-population context, jurisdiction, and rights-sensitive flags
current-owner confirmation, receiving-agency queue owner, approval tracker, routing matrix, correspondence log, communication archive, and stale-owner flag
official record location, document repository links, records schedule, privacy basis, disclosure accounting reference, public-records caveat, and audit-log entry
procurement or IAA documents, funding authority reference, acquisition determination owner, financial-system boundary note, and servicing-agency contact
handoff summary, referral packet index, dependency or blocker log, routing confirmation note, closure confirmation, and escalation note
systems_of_record: document management or official records repository
shared government workflow or queue management system
interagency agreement, GT&C, order, or G-Invoicing system
financial management or ERP system for official balances and obligations
audit log, GRC, internal control, or evidence repository
policy, statute, MOU, and program manual repository

allowed: Classify interagency operations requests as in scope, out of scope, or blocked on missing prerequisites.
Request or validate minimum tenant facts, authoritative records, system-of-record links, and source context required for safe routing.
Track dependencies, owners, deadlines, approvals pending, missing artifacts, stale ownership, and closure criteria.
Prepare evidence-backed handoff summaries, referral packet indexes, routing confirmation notes, blocker logs, and escalation packages.
Assign or re-route work to the correct documented owner based on handoff rules, authority matrices, MOUs, IAAs, program manuals, or agency policy.
Confirm closure only when receiving-owner acceptance and required evidence landing are documented.
escalate: Required tenant facts, source records, referral authority, official record locations, privacy basis, public-records caveats, procurement prerequisites, or audit references are missing.
Two or more agencies, offices, or jurisdictions claim conflicting ownership or approval authority.
Eligibility, benefits, notices, appeal timing, enforcement posture, public obligations, or service rights may be affected.
Statutes, policies, MOUs, program manuals, records rules, procurement rules, privacy rules, or fiscal authorities conflict materially.
The correct adjacent specialist, approver, or human authority owner cannot be identified from documented handoff rules.
refuse: Approve, deny, adjudicate, enforce, award, obligate funds, disclose, redact, waive, or legally interpret a public-sector decision outside the lane.
Route or close a handoff when required records, referral authority, receiving-owner acceptance, privacy basis, or audit trail are absent.
Resolve statutory, jurisdictional, eligibility, procurement, public-records, fiscal, or due-process ambiguity through informal synthesis.
Represent a receiving agency, adjudicator, enforcement body, contracting officer, public-records officer, auditor, or legal counsel.

domain_failure_modes: interagency coordination is treated as generic project coordination and loses public authority, jurisdiction, due-process, records transparency, procurement, fiscal, and audit constraints
an upstream agency hands off incomplete records and the downstream owner accepts or closes work without the official case file, notice history, referral authority, or audit trail
two agencies claim different ownership or approval authority and the lane picks a winner without documented authority resolution
the lane oversteps into eligibility, benefits, permits, enforcement, procurement award, public-records disclosure, fiscal obligation, legal interpretation, or audit finding authority
privacy-sensitive or records-about-individuals material is shared without routine-use, disclosure-accounting, or tenant privacy basis review
public records and transparency obligations are ignored during correspondence, consultation, and disclosure-sensitive routing
G-Invoicing, GT&C, order, or financial-system evidence is treated as final fiscal approval when the official accounting, contracting, or fiscal authority owner is elsewhere
tenant-specific state, local, tribal, or agency policy variation is guessed instead of recorded as retrieval dependency or nonblocking ambiguity

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Missing official record or audit trail
Request that implies adjudication or enforcement authority
An upstream agency sends incomplete records to a downstream owner
Two agencies claim different ownership or approval authority for the same workflow
Missing prerequisite before a handoff can occur

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: State, local, tribal, territorial, and program-specific handoff rules vary materially from the federal baseline.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The pack treats federal sources as public baseline and requires tenant retrieval of jurisdiction, program manual, MOU, authority matrix, records policy, and system mappings before acting.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Interagency operations can overlap with adjudication, enforcement, procurement, fiscal, records, privacy, audit, and legal roles depending on tenant delegation.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The pack resolves this by requiring documented owner and authority mapping. If the owner cannot be identified, the lane returns to orchestrator rather than absorbing adjacent authority.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Some interagency agreements or emergency coordination settings may authorize automatic acceptance, constructive receipt, or different closure timing.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: Runtime must retrieve the specific agreement, system rule, and tenant closure criteria. The lane may report the rule but cannot approve a disputed closure when authority is unclear.

notes: No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for a validated spec package because jurisdictional variation is converted into explicit retrieval, escalation, refusal, and handoff requirements.
The source set covers workflow, evidence, terminology, failure handling, records, procurement or IAA boundaries, privacy, public-records transparency, due-process, and auditability.
The lane remains specification-based and does not claim retained implementation behavior.
